sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 96:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 96:1
items.html: Because with respect to the vessel itself, there is only one refinement and one departure here. This is so because in the end, there is only a vessel Malchut here, and no vessels were added to the vessel Malchut by all those departures. The ten Sefirot that we discern in it are like one long vessel that contains ten parts that are not equal to each other. (Item 22, and Inner Light there)
